<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Added missing null check in "CollectionUtils#addIgnoreNull(Collection, Object)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-400">COLLECTIONS-400</a>. Thanks to Shin Hwei Tan.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added new method "get(int)" to "CircularFifoQueue". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-399">COLLECTIONS-399</a>. Thanks to Sebb.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added "LazyIteratorChain" iterator. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-396">COLLECTIONS-396</a>. Thanks to Jeff Rodriguez.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added "ListUtils#partition" method to split a List into consecutive sublists. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-393">COLLECTIONS-393</a>. Thanks to Chris Shayan.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Fixed Javadoc for "MapUtils#toProperties(Map)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-391">COLLECTIONS-391</a>. Thanks to Shin Hwei Tan.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Clarified Javadoc for "TransformerUtils#mapTransformer" for null input. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-389">COLLECTIONS-389</a>. Thanks to Shin Hwei Tan.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Clarified Javadoc for "FactoryUtils#prototypeFactory" for null input. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-388">COLLECTIONS-388</a>. Thanks to Shin Hwei Tan.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Fixed inconsistent Javadoc for "MapUtils#synchronizedMap(Map)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-384">COLLECTIONS-384</a>. Thanks to Shin Hwei Tan.</td>
<td><a href="#team--ggregory">ggregory</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added "CollectionUtils#forAllDo" implementation which takes an "Iterator" as input. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-383">COLLECTIONS-383</a>. Thanks to Adrian Cumiskey.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>Change maven coordinates to "org.apache.commons.commons-collections4". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-382">COLLECTIONS-382</a>. Thanks to Olivier Lamy.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>Move the project structure to a standard maven layout. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-381">COLLECTIONS-381</a>. Thanks to Olivier Lamy.</td>
<td><a href="#team--sebb">sebb</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Fixed infinite loop when calling "UnmodifiableBoundedCollection#unmodifiableBoundedCollection()". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-380">COLLECTIONS-380</a>. Thanks to Dave Brosius.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Fixed Javadoc for several methods wrt expected NullPointerExceptions. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-379">COLLECTIONS-379</a>. Thanks to Shin Hwei Tan.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added method "ListUtils#defaultIfNull(List, List)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-375">COLLECTIONS-375</a>. Thanks to Ivan Hristov.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>TransformingComparator now supports different types for its input/output values. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-372">COLLECTIONS-372</a>.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"DualTreeBidiMap" now uses the correct comparator for the reverse map during de-serialization. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-364">COLLECTIONS-364</a>.</td>
<td><a href="#team--sebb">sebb</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"TransformedMap" in the package "splitmap" can now be serialized. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-363">COLLECTIONS-363</a>.</td>
<td><a href="#team--sebb">sebb</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>"CollectionUtils#filter(Iterable, Predicate)" will now return whether the collection
      has been modified. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-362">COLLECTIONS-362</a>. Thanks to Jean-Noel Rouvignac.</td>
<td><a href="#team--brentworden">brentworden</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Add method "CollectionUtils#filterInverse(Iterable, Predicate)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-361">COLLECTIONS-361</a>. Thanks to Jean-Noel Rouvignac.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"FilterListIterator#hasNext" does not throw a NullPointerException anymore
      to comply to the Java iterator specification. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-360">COLLECTIONS-360</a>. Thanks to Sai Zhang.</td>
<td><a href="#team--jochen">jochen</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"ListUtils#intersection(List, List)" will now also work correctly if there
      are duplicate elements in the provided lists. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-359">COLLECTIONS-359</a>. Thanks to Mark Shead.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"AbstractCollectionDecorator" will now use internally "decorated()" to access
      the decorated collection. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-352">COLLECTIONS-352</a>. Thanks to Adam Gent.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Remove" src="assets/images/remove_7f9c320d5dd037d3.gif" title="Remove"/></td>
<td>Removed features which are now supported by the JDK. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-351">COLLECTIONS-351</a>. Thanks to Henri Yandell.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Removed debug output in "MapUtils#getNumber(Map)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-350">COLLECTIONS-350</a>. Thanks to Michael Akerman.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Fixed Javadoc for all "transformedXXX(XXX)" methods in the respective Utils classes
      to clarify that existing objects in the list are not transformed. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-348">COLLECTIONS-348</a>. Thanks to Paul Benedict.</td>
<td><a href="#team--brentworden">brentworden</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Singleton classes in package "functors" are now correctly de-serialized. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-343">COLLECTIONS-343</a>. Thanks to Goran Hacek.</td>
<td><a href="#team--mbenson">mbenson</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>"NOPClosure" is now a final class. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-341">COLLECTIONS-341</a>. Thanks to Goran Hacek.</td>
<td><a href="#team--mbenson">mbenson</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Removed broken methods "equals(Object)" and "hashCode()" in class "NOPClosure". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-340">COLLECTIONS-340</a>. Thanks to Goran Hacek.</td>
<td><a href="#team--mbenson">mbenson</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Simplified exceptions as the cause is available from the parent. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-336">COLLECTIONS-336</a>. Thanks to sebb.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Fixed cache assignment for "TreeBidiMap#entrySet". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-335">COLLECTIONS-335</a>. Thanks to sebb.</td>
<td><a href="#team--jochen">jochen</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Synchronized access to lock in "StaticBucketMap#size()". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-334">COLLECTIONS-334</a>. Thanks to sebb.</td>
<td><a href="#team--jochen">jochen</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Added clarification to Javadoc of "ListOrderedMap" that "IdentityMap" and
      "CaseInsensitiveMap" are not supported. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-332">COLLECTIONS-332</a>. Thanks to Tom Parker.</td>
<td><a href="#team--jochen">jochen</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Improve Javadoc of "CollatingIterator" wrt the used "Comparator" and throw a
      NullPointerException in "CollatingIterator#least" if no comparator is set. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-331">COLLECTIONS-331</a>. Thanks to Michael Krkoska.</td>
<td><a href="#team--jochen">jochen</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"LRUMap#keySet()#remove(Object)" will not throw a "ConcurrentModificationException" anymore. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-330">COLLECTIONS-330</a>. Thanks to Joerg Schaible.</td>
<td><a href="#team--mbenson">mbenson</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Improved performance of "ListUtils#intersection(List, List)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-328">COLLECTIONS-328</a>. Thanks to Thomas Rogan, Jilles van Gurp.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added serialVersionUID fields for "CompositeCollection", "CompositeSet",
      "EmptyMapMutator", "EmptySetMutator". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-327">COLLECTIONS-327</a>. Thanks to sebb.</td>
<td><a href="#team--brentworden">brentworden</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>Fields transformer and decorated in class "TransformingComparator" are now final. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-324">COLLECTIONS-324</a>. Thanks to sebb.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Changed behavior of "CaseInsensitiveMap" constructor to be compliant with "HashMap"
      in case the initial capacity is set to zero. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-323">COLLECTIONS-323</a>. Thanks to Maarten Brak.</td>
<td><a href="#team--jochen">jochen</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added NodeListIterator and convenience methods in IteratorUtils to iterate over
      an org.w3c.dom.NodeList. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-322">COLLECTIONS-322</a>. Thanks to Thomas Vahrst.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Improved performance of "StaticBucketMap#putAll(Map)" by iterating over the entry set. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-320">COLLECTIONS-320</a>. Thanks to sebb.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Avoid redundant null check in "IteratorUtils#getIterator(Object)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-319">COLLECTIONS-319</a>. Thanks to sebb.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Use a private method to populate the object in "AbstractHashedMap(Map)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-317">COLLECTIONS-317</a>. Thanks to sebb.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Fixed Javadoc of "LRUMap" wrt to the maxSize parameter of the constructor. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-316">COLLECTIONS-316</a>. Thanks to ori.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added new abstract class "CatchAndRethrowClosure" that re-throws any checked exception
      as unchecked "FunctorException". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-313">COLLECTIONS-313</a>. Thanks to David J. M. Karlsen.</td>
<td><a href="#team--brentworden">brentworden</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Use of final keyword where applicable, minor performance improvements by properly
      initializing the capacity of newly created collections when known in advance. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-312">COLLECTIONS-312</a>. Thanks to Peter Lawrey, Gary Gregory.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>"SetUniqueList#subList()" will now return an unmodifiable list as changes to it
      may invalidate the parent list. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-307">COLLECTIONS-307</a>. Thanks to Christian Semrau, Thomas Vahrst.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"SetUniqueList#subList()#contains(Object)" will now correctly check the subList
      rather than the parent list. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-307">COLLECTIONS-307</a>. Thanks to Christian Semrau.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added method "CollectionUtils#subtract(Iterable, Iterable, Predicate)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-306">COLLECTIONS-306</a>. Thanks to Chris Shayan.</td>
<td><a href="#team--brentworden">brentworden</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"SetUniqueList#set(int, Object)" will now correctly enforce the uniqueness constraint. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-304">COLLECTIONS-304</a>. Thanks to Rafał Figas, Bjorn Townsend.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Improved Javadoc for "Unmodifiable*" classes wrt behavior when the user tries
      to modify the collection. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-303">COLLECTIONS-303</a>. Thanks to Emmanuel Bourg.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>Calling "CollectionUtils#sizeIsEmpty(null)" will now return true. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-298">COLLECTIONS-298</a>. Thanks to Benjamin Bentmann.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added methods "CollectionUtils#collate(...)" to merge two sorted Collections
      into a sorted List using the standard O(n) merge algorithm. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-296">COLLECTIONS-296</a>. Thanks to Julius Davies.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"CaseInsensitiveMap" will now convert input strings to lower-case in a
      locale-independent manner. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-294">COLLECTIONS-294</a>. Thanks to Benjamin Bentmann.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added support for using custom "Equator" objects in "EqualPredicate". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-293">COLLECTIONS-293</a>. Thanks to Stephen Kestle.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added method "CollatingIterator#getIteratorIndex()". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-289">COLLECTIONS-289</a>. Thanks to Fredrik Kjellberg.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Fixed Javadoc for "ListUtils#transformedList(List)" to clarify that existing objects
      in the list are not transformed. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-256,COLLECTIONS-288">COLLECTIONS-256,COLLECTIONS-288</a>. Thanks to Paul Benedict.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added method "CollectionUtils#extractSingleton(Collection)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-286">COLLECTIONS-286</a>. Thanks to Geoffrey De Smet.</td>
<td><a href="#team--mbenson">mbenson</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added serialization support for "TreeBidiMap". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-285">COLLECTIONS-285</a>. Thanks to Christian Gruenberg.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>The predicate that rejected an object to be added to a "PredicatedCollection"
      is now contained in the respective exception message. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-280">COLLECTIONS-280</a>. Thanks to Chris Lewis.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added "IndexedCollection" collection decorator which provides a map-like
      view on an existing collection. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-275">COLLECTIONS-275</a>. Thanks to Stephen Kestle.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added serialization support for "FixedOrderComparator" and "TransformingComparator". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-272">COLLECTIONS-272</a>. Thanks to Chaitanya Mutyala.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"MultiKey" will now be correctly serialized/de-serialized. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-266">COLLECTIONS-266</a>. Thanks to Joerg Schaible.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>"TreeBag" will now only accept "Comparable" objects as input when used with natural ordering. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-265">COLLECTIONS-265</a>. Thanks to David Saff.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added methods "MapUtils#populateMap(MultiMap, ...)" to support also "MultiMap" instances
      as input. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-263">COLLECTIONS-263</a>. Thanks to John Hunsley.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Fixed Javadoc for methods "firstKey()" and "lastKey()" in class "AbstractLinkedMap". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-262">COLLECTIONS-262</a>. Thanks to Lisen Mu.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"Flat3Map#remove(Object)" will now return the correct value mapped to the removed key
      if the size of the map is less or equal 3. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-261">COLLECTIONS-261</a>. Thanks to ori.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added constructor "TransformingComparator(Transformer)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-260">COLLECTIONS-260</a>. Thanks to Stephen Kestle.</td>
<td><a href="#team--mbenson">mbenson</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added "DualLinkedHashBidiMap" bidi map implementation. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-258">COLLECTIONS-258</a>. Thanks to Nathan Blomquist.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Removed unused variables in "TreeBidiMap". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-255">COLLECTIONS-255</a>. Thanks to Henri Yandell.</td>
<td><a href="#team--mbenson">mbenson</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>The static factory methods have been renamed from "getInstance()" to a camel-case
      version of the class name, e.g. "truePredicate()" for class "TruePredicate". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-251,COLLECTIONS-321">COLLECTIONS-251,COLLECTIONS-321</a>. Thanks to Stephen Kestle.</td>
<td><a href="#team--mbenson">mbenson</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"SetUniqueList.addAll(int, Collection)" now correctly add the collection at the
      provided index. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-249">COLLECTIONS-249</a>. Thanks to Joe Kelly.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added "Equator" interface. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-242">COLLECTIONS-242</a>.</td>
<td><a href="#team--skestle">skestle</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added "PassiveExpiringMap" map decorator. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-241">COLLECTIONS-241</a>. Thanks to Elifarley Callado Coelho.</td>
<td><a href="#team--brentworden">brentworden</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>"MultiValueMap" is now serializable. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-240">COLLECTIONS-240</a>. Thanks to Wouter de Vaal.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added method "MultiValueMap#iterator()" to return a flattened version of
      "entrySet().iterator()". Clarified Javadoc for "entrySet()" that the returned Entry
      objects are unflattened, i.e. the Entry object for a given key contains all values
      mapped to this key. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-237">COLLECTIONS-237</a>. Thanks to Nils Kaiser, Alan Mehlo.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added method "ListUtils#indexOf(List, Predicate)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-235">COLLECTIONS-235</a>. Thanks to Nathan Egge.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Fixed several unit tests which were using parameters to "assertEquals(...)" in wrong order. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-232">COLLECTIONS-232</a>. Thanks to Mark Hindess.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>Return concrete class in static factory methods instead of base class interface
      (except for Unmodifiable decorators). Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-231">COLLECTIONS-231</a>. Thanks to Torsten Curdt.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>"CollectionUtils#size(Collection)" now returns 0 when called with null as input. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-230,COLLECTIONS-297,COLLECTIONS-318">COLLECTIONS-230,COLLECTIONS-297,COLLECTIONS-318</a>. Thanks to Stepan Koltsov, sebb.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Remove" src="assets/images/remove_7f9c320d5dd037d3.gif" title="Remove"/></td>
<td>Removed deprecated classes and methods. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-229">COLLECTIONS-229</a>.</td>
<td><a href="#team--scolebourne">scolebourne</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"MultiValueMap#put(Object, Object)" and "MultiValueMap#putAll(Object, Collection)"
      now correctly return if the map has changed by this operation. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-228">COLLECTIONS-228</a>.</td>
<td><a href="#team--scolebourne">scolebourne</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added method "ListOrderedMap#putAll(int, Map)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-226">COLLECTIONS-226</a>. Thanks to Vasily Ivanov.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added new "Trie" interface with a first concrete implementation "PatriciaTrie"
      together with decorators "Unmodifiable" and "Synchronized". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-225">COLLECTIONS-225</a>. Thanks to Sam Berlin, Roger Kapsi.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>"CollectionUtils#addAll(...)" methods now return if the collection has been changed
      by this operation. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-223">COLLECTIONS-223</a>. Thanks to Vasily Ivanov.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>"CompositeCollection", "CompositeMap" and "CompositeSet" are now serializable. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-221">COLLECTIONS-221</a>. Thanks to Pal Denes.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"CollectionUtils#removeAll" wrongly called "ListUtils#retainAll". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-219">COLLECTIONS-219</a>. Thanks to Tom Leccese.</td>
<td><a href="#team--scolebourne">scolebourne</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>The "CollectionUtils#select(Collection, Predicate, Collection)" method will now
      return the output collection. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-218">COLLECTIONS-218</a>.</td>
<td><a href="#team--skestle">skestle</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Calling "setValue(Object)" on any Entry returned by a "Flat3Map" will now
      correctly set the value for the current entry. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-217">COLLECTIONS-217</a>. Thanks to Matt Bishop.</td>
<td><a href="#team--scolebourne">scolebourne</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"MultiKey#toString()" will now use "Arrays#toString(List)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-216">COLLECTIONS-216</a>. Thanks to Hendrik Maryns.</td>
<td><a href="#team--scolebourne">scolebourne</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added support for resettable iterators in "IteratorIterable". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-213">COLLECTIONS-213</a>. Thanks to Dusan Chromy.</td>
<td><a href="#team--brentworden">brentworden</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added methods "MapUtils#populateMap(Map, Iterable, Transformer, ...)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-194">COLLECTIONS-194</a>. Thanks to Dave Meikle.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>"CollectionUtils#forAllDo(Collection, Closure)" now returns the provided closure. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-182">COLLECTIONS-182</a>. Thanks to Jim Cakalic.</td>
<td><a href="#team--mbenson">mbenson</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>Make generic versions of all classes in collections. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-110,COLLECTIONS-243,COLLECTIONS-245,COLLECTIONS-247,                    COLLECTIONS-253,COLLECTIONS-273,COLLECTIONS-282">COLLECTIONS-110,COLLECTIONS-243,COLLECTIONS-245,COLLECTIONS-247,                    COLLECTIONS-253,COLLECTIONS-273,COLLECTIONS-282</a>.</td>
<td><a href="#team--multiple">multiple</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Add" src="assets/images/add_833d51a6c4928afd.gif" title="Add"/></td>
<td>Added class "ComparatorPredicate". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-8">COLLECTIONS-8</a>. Thanks to Rune Peter Bjørnstad.</td>
<td><a href="#team--brentworden">brentworden</a></td></tr></table></section><section><a id="a3.2.2"></a>
<h2>Release 3.2.2 – 2015-11-15</h2>
<table>
<tr>
<th>Type</th>
<th>Changes</th>
<th>By</th></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Update" src="assets/images/update_f3d392c27550ec36.gif" title="Update"/></td>
<td>Serialization support for unsafe classes in the functor package is disabled
      by default as this can be exploited for remote code execution attacks.
      To re-enable the feature the system property "org.apache.commons.collections.enableUnsafeSerialization"
      needs to be set to "true".
      Classes considered to be unsafe are: CloneTransformer, ForClosure, InstantiateFactory,
      InstantiateTransformer, InvokerTransformer, PrototypeCloneFactory,
      PrototypeSerializationFactory, WhileClosure. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-580">COLLECTIONS-580</a>.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"ExtendedProperties" will now use a privileged action to access the
      "file.separator" system property. In case the class does not have
      permission to read system properties, the "File#separator" field will
      be used instead. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-538">COLLECTIONS-538</a>. Thanks to Trejkaz.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Tree traversal with a TreeListIterator will not be affected anymore by
      the removal of an element directly after a call to previous(). Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-447">COLLECTIONS-447</a>. Thanks to Jeffrey Barnes.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>SetUniqueList.set(int, Object) now works correctly if the object to be inserted
      is already placed at the given position. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-444">COLLECTIONS-444</a>. Thanks to Thomas Vahrst, John Vasileff.</td>
<td><a href="#team--tn">tn</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Removed debug output in "MapUtils#getNumber(Map)". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-350">COLLECTIONS-350</a>. Thanks to Michael Akerman.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Fixed cache assignment for "TreeBidiMap#entrySet". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-335">COLLECTIONS-335</a>. Thanks to sebb.</td>
<td><a href="#team--jochen">jochen</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>Synchronized access to lock in "StaticBucketMap#size()". Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-334">COLLECTIONS-334</a>. Thanks to sebb.</td>
<td><a href="#team--jochen">jochen</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"SetUniqueList#subList()#contains(Object)" will now correctly check the subList
      rather than the parent list. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-307">COLLECTIONS-307</a>. Thanks to Christian Semrau.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"SetUniqueList#set(int, Object)" will now correctly enforce the uniqueness constraint. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-304">COLLECTIONS-304</a>. Thanks to Rafał Figas, Bjorn Townsend.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>
<td>"CaseInsensitiveMap" will now convert input strings to lower-case in a
      locale-independent manner. Fixes <a href="https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-294">COLLECTIONS-294</a>. Thanks to Benjamin Bentmann.</td>
<td><a href="#team--bayard">bayard</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td><img alt="Fix" src="assets/images/fix_4a691bebe5f8bb38.gif" title="Fix"/></td>

<a id="issue-management"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-collections/issue-management.html -->

<!-- page_index: 3 -->

# Overview

This project uses [JIRA](http://www.atlassian.com/software/jira).

# Issue Management

Issues, bugs, and feature requests should be submitted to the following issue management system for this project.

```
https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS
```

---

<a id="dependency-info"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-collections/dependency-info.html -->

<!-- page_index: 4 -->

# Maven Coordinates

## Apache Maven

```
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.apache.commons</groupId>
  <artifactId>commons-collections4</artifactId>
  <version>4.5.0</version>
</dependency>
```

## Apache Ivy

```
<dependency org="org.apache.commons" name="commons-collections4" rev="4.5.0">
  <artifact name="commons-collections4" type="jar" />
</dependency>
```

## Groovy Grape

```
@Grapes(
@Grab(group='org.apache.commons', module='commons-collections4', version='4.5.0')
)
```

## Gradle/Grails

```
implementation 'org.apache.commons:commons-collections4:4.5.0'
```

## Scala SBT

```
libraryDependencies += "org.apache.commons" % "commons-collections4" % "4.5.0"
```

## Leiningen

```
[org.apache.commons/commons-collections4 "4.5.0"]
```

<a id="scm"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-collections/scm.html -->

<!-- page_index: 5 -->

# Overview

This project uses [Git](https://git-scm.com/) to manage its source code. Instructions on Git use can be found at <https://git-scm.com/doc>.

# Web Browser Access

The following is a link to a browsable version of the source repository:

```
https://gitbox.apache.org/repos/asf?p=commons-collections.git
```

# Anonymous Access

The source can be checked out anonymously from Git with this command (See <https://git-scm.com/docs/git-clone>):

```
$ git clone http://gitbox.apache.org/repos/asf/commons-collections.git
```

# Developer Access

Only project developers can access the Git tree via this method (See <https://git-scm.com/docs/git-clone>).

```
$ git clone https://gitbox.apache.org/repos/asf/commons-collections.git
```

# Access from Behind a Firewall

Refer to the documentation of the SCM used for more information about access behind a firewall.

<a id="security"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-collections/security.html -->

<!-- page_index: 6 -->

# General Information

For information about reporting or asking questions about
security problems, please see the [security page
of the Commons project](https://commons.apache.org/security.html).

# Apache Commons Collections Security Vulnerabilities

This page lists all security vulnerabilities fixed in
released versions of Apache Commons Collections. Each
vulnerability is given a security impact rating by the
development team - please note that this rating may vary from
platform to platform. We also list the versions of Commons
Collections the flaw is known to affect, and where a flaw has not
been verified list the version with a question mark.

Please note that binary patches are never provided. If you
need to apply a source code patch, use the building
instructions for the Commons Collections version that you are
using.

If you need help on building Commons Collections or other help
on following the instructions to mitigate the known
vulnerabilities listed here, please send your questions to the
public [Collections Users mailing
list](https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-collections/mail-lists.html).

If you have encountered an unlisted security vulnerability
or other unexpected behavior that has security impact, or if
the descriptions here are incomplete, please report them
privately to the Apache Security Team. Thank you.

## Fixed in Apache Commons Collections 3.2.2 and 4.1

**High: Remote Code Execution during object de-serialization**

The Apache Commons Collections library contains various classes
in the "functor" package which are serializable and use reflection.
This can be exploited for remote code execution attacks by injecting
specially crafted objects to applications that de-serialize
java objects from untrusted sources and have the Apache Commons Collections
library in their classpath and do not perform any kind of input
validation.

The implemented fix can be tracked via its related issue
[COLLECTIONS-580](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/COLLECTIONS-580):

- **3.2.2**: de-serialization of unsafe classes in the functor package
  will trigger an "UnsupportedOperationException" by default. In order to re-enable
  the previous behavior, the system property
  "org.apache.commons.collections.enableUnsafeSerialization" has to be set to "true".
- **4.1**: de-serialization support for unsafe classes in the functor package
  has been completely removed (unsafe classes do not implement Serializable anymore).

The potential exploit was first presented at AppSecCali2015 [3] on 28 January 2015 by
Gabriel Lawrence and Chris Frohoff. Based on these exploits, Stephen Breen published
on 06 November 2015 attack scenarios [4] for various products like WebSphere, JBoss, Jenkins, WebLogic, and OpenNMS. The Security team was **not** informed about these security
problems prior to their publication. No CVE id was assigned for the Apache Commons
Collections library, please refer to [1] or [2] for more information about the general
problem with Java serialization.

Affects: 3.0 - 4.0

Related links:

1. Vulnerability Report for Oracle Weblogic Server:
   [CVE-2015-4852](https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2015-4852)
2. Vulnerability Report for Red Hat JBoss products:
   [CVE-2015-7501](https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2015-7501)
   ([Red Hat Portal](https://access.redhat.com/security/cve/cve-2015-7501))
3. Apache Commons
   [statement](https://blogs.apache.org/foundation/entry/apache_commons_statement_to_widespread)
   to widespread Java object de-serialization vulnerability
4. [Presentation](https://www.slideshare.net/frohoff1/appseccali-2015-marshalling-pickles) @ AppSecCali2015 by Lawrence and Frohoff
5. [Attack scenarios](https://foxglovesecurity.com/2015/11/06/what-do-weblogic-websphere-jboss-jenkins-opennms-and-your-application-have-in-common-this-vulnerability/)
   for various products by Stephen Breen

# Errors and Omissions

Please report any errors or omissions to [the dev mailing list](https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-collections/mail-lists.html).

---

<a id="userguide"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-collections/userguide.html -->

<!-- page_index: 7 -->

# User guide

Commons-Collections provides a large number of classes to aid day to day programming.
This document highlights some key features to get you started.

- [Utilities](#userguide--utilities) for the standard collections.
- [Maps](#userguide--maps)
  - [Map Iteration](#userguide--map.2520iteration)
  - [Ordered Maps](#userguide--ordered.2520maps)
  - [Bidirectional Maps](#userguide--bidirectional.2520maps)
- [Bags](#userguide--bags)
- [Bloom filters (starting in 4.5.0)](https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-collections/bloomFilters.html)

## Note On Synchronization

Commons-collections uses a design approach to synchronization similar
to the standard Java collections. The majority of the various implementations
of collections, maps and bags are not thread safe without additional
synchronization. The appropriate `synchronizeXXX` method on `Collections` is one way that these implementations can be synchronized for use in a
multithreaded application.

The class level javadocs should indicate whether a particular
implementation is safe for multithreaded access without additional
synchronization. Where there is no explicit indication that the implementation
is thread safe then it should be assumed that synchronization is required.
Please report the missing documentation to the commons development team.

# Utilities

A Utility class is provided for each major collection interface.
Thus, the `Set` and `SortedSet` interfaces are provided for by `SetUtils.`
These classes provide useful methods for working with that collection type.

The most methods are found on the two 'root' collection utility classes -
`CollectionUtils` and `MapUtils.`
As all other collection interfaces extend `Collection` or `Map` these utilities can be used widely.
They include intersection, counting, iteration, functor and typecasting operations amongst others.
The utility classes also provide access to collection decorator classes in a way similar to the JDK `Collections` class.

# Maps

## Map Iteration

The JDK `Map` interface always suffered from being difficult to iterate over.
API users are forced to either iterate over an EntrySet or over the KeySet.
Commons-Collections now provides a new interface - `MapIterator` that allows simple iteration over maps.

```

IterableMap map = new HashedMap();
MapIterator it = map.mapIterator();
while (it.hasNext()) {
  Object key = it.next();
  Object value = it.getValue();

  it.setValue(newValue);
}
```

## Ordered Maps

A new interface is provided for maps that have an order but are not sorted - `OrderedMap.`
Two implementations are provided - `LinkedMap` and `ListOrderedMap` (a decorator).
This interface supports the map iterator, and also allows iteration both forwards and backwards through the map.

```

OrderedMap map = new LinkedMap();
map.put("FIVE", "5");
map.put("SIX", "6");
map.put("SEVEN", "7");
map.firstKey();  // returns "FIVE"
map.nextKey("FIVE");  // returns "SIX"
map.nextKey("SIX");  // returns "SEVEN"
```

## Bidirectional Maps

A new interface hierarchy has been added to support bidirectional maps - `BidiMap.`
These represent maps where the key can lookup the value and the value can look up the key with equal ease.

```

BidiMap bidi = new TreeBidiMap();
bidi.put("SIX", "6");
bidi.get("SIX");  // returns "6"
bidi.getKey("6");  // returns "SIX"
bidi.removeValue("6");  // removes the mapping
BidiMap inverse = bidi.inverseBidiMap();  // returns a map with keys and values swapped
```

Additional interfaces are provided for ordered and sorted bidirectional maps.
Implementations are provided for each bidirectional map type.

# Bags

A new interface hierarchy has been added to support bags - `Bag.`
These represent collections where a certain number of copies of each element is held.

```

Bag bag = new HashBag();
bag.add("ONE", 6);  // add 6 copies of "ONE"
bag.remove("ONE", 2);  // removes 2 copies of "ONE"
bag.getCount("ONE");  // returns 4, the number of copies in the bag (6 - 2)
```

Implementations are provided for both unsorted and sorted Bags.

---

This document provides an overview of the various documents and links that are part of this project's general information. All of this content is automatically generated by [Maven](https://maven.apache.org) on behalf of the project.

# Project Team

A successful project requires many people to play many roles. Some members write code or documentation, while others are valuable as testers, submitting patches and suggestions.

The project team is comprised of Members and Contributors. Members have direct access to the source of a project and actively evolve the code-base. Contributors improve the project through submission of patches and suggestions to the Members. The number of Contributors to the project is unbounded. Get involved today. All contributions to the project are greatly appreciated.

<li>com.google.guava:failureaccess:jar:1.0.2 (test) <img alt="[Information]" id="_img43" src="assets/images/icon-info-sml_c6419c8b33f30224.gif"/><div id="_dep42">
<table>
<tr>
<th>Guava InternalFutureFailureAccess and InternalFutures</th></tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p><b>Description: </b>Contains
    com.google.common.util.concurrent.internal.InternalFutureFailureAccess and
    InternalFutures. Most users will never need to use this artifact. Its
    classes are conceptually a part of Guava, but they're in this separate
    artifact so that Android libraries can use them without pulling in all of
    Guava (just as they can use ListenableFuture by depending on the
    listenablefuture artifact).</p>
<p><b>URL: </b><a href="https://github.com/google/guava/failureaccess">https://github.com/google/guava/failureaccess</a></p>
<p><b>Project Licenses: </b><a href="http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.txt">The Apache Software License, Version 2.0</a></p></td></tr></table></div></li>
<li>com.google.guava:listenablefuture:jar:9999.0-empty-to-avoid-conflict-with-guava (test) <img alt="[Information]" id="_img45" src="assets/images/icon-info-sml_c6419c8b33f30224.gif"/><div id="_dep44">
<table>
<tr>
<th>Guava ListenableFuture only</th></tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p><b>Description: </b>An empty artifact that Guava depends on to signal that it is providing
    ListenableFuture -- but is also available in a second "version" that
    contains com.google.common.util.concurrent.ListenableFuture class, without
    any other Guava classes. The idea is:

    - If users want only ListenableFuture, they depend on listenablefuture-1.0.

    - If users want all of Guava, they depend on guava, which, as of Guava
    27.0, depends on
    listenablefuture-9999.0-empty-to-avoid-conflict-with-guava. The 9999.0-...
    version number is enough for some build systems (notably, Gradle) to select
    that empty artifact over the "real" listenablefuture-1.0 -- avoiding a
    conflict with the copy of ListenableFuture in guava itself. If users are
    using an older version of Guava or a build system other than Gradle, they
    may see class conflicts. If so, they can solve them by manually excluding
    the listenablefuture artifact or manually forcing their build systems to
    use 9999.0-....</p>
<p><b>URL: </b><a href="https://github.com/google/guava/listenablefuture">https://github.com/google/guava/listenablefuture</a></p>
<p><b>Project Licenses: </b><a href="http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.txt">The Apache Software License, Version 2.0</a></p></td></tr></table></div></li></ul></li>
<li>junit:junit:jar:4.13.2 (test) <img alt="[Information]" id="_img47" src="assets/images/icon-info-sml_c6419c8b33f30224.gif"/><div id="_dep46">

<!-- page_index: 14 -->

# Overview

This project uses [GitHub Actions](https://github.com/features/actions/).

# Access

The following is a link to the continuous integration system used by the project:

```
https://github.com/apache/commons-parent/actions
```

# Notifiers

No notifiers are defined. Please check back at a later date.

<https://repository.apache.org/service/local/staging/deploy/maven2>

<a id="project-reports"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-collections/project-reports.html -->

<!-- page_index: 16 -->

# Generated Reports

This document provides an overview of the various reports that are automatically generated by [Maven](https://maven.apache.org) . Each report is briefly described below.

## Overview

| Document | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Changes](#changes) | Changes report on releases of this project. |
| [JIRA Report](#jira-changes) | Report on Issues from the JIRA Issue Tracking System. |
| [Javadoc](https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-collections/apidocs/index.html) | Javadoc API documentation. |
| [Source Xref](https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-collections/xref/index.html) | HTML based, cross-reference version of Java source code. |
| [Test Source Xref](https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-collections/xref-test/index.html) | HTML based, cross-reference version of Java test source code. |
| [Surefire](#surefire) | Report on the test results of the project. |
| [Rat Report](#rat-report) | Report on compliance to license related source code policies |
| [JaCoCo](https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-collections/jacoco/index.html) | JaCoCo Coverage Report. |
| [japicmp](#japicmp) | Comparing source compatibility of commons-collections4-4.5.0.jar against commons-collections4-4.4.jar |
| [Checkstyle](#checkstyle) | Report on coding style conventions. |
| [SpotBugs](#spotbugs) | Generates a source code report with the SpotBugs Library. |
| [CPD](#cpd) | Duplicate code detection. |
| [PMD](#pmd) | Verification of coding rules. |

---

<td>Exception thrown in class org.apache.commons.collections4.bidimap.TreeBidiMap at new org.apache.commons.collections4.bidimap.TreeBidiMap(Map) will leave the constructor. The object under construction remains partially initialized and may be vulnerable to Finalizer attacks.</td>

# CPD Results

<table class="layout-table">
<tr>
<td>
</td>
<td>
<section><a id="CPD_Results"></a>
<h1>CPD Results</h1>
<p>The following document contains the results of PMD's  <a href="https://pmd.github.io/latest/pmd_userdocs_cpd.html">CPD</a> 7.8.0.</p><section><a id="Duplications"></a>
<h2>Duplications</h2>
<table>
<tr>
<th>File</th>
<th>Line</th></tr>
<tr>
<td>org/apache/commons/collections4/list/AbstractLinkedList.java</td>
<td><a href="https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-collections/xref/org/apache/commons/collections4/list/AbstractLinkedList.html#L615">615</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td>org/apache/commons/collections4/list/AbstractLinkedListJava21.java</td>
<td><a href="https://commons.apache.org/proper/commons-collections/xref/org/apache/commons/collections4/list/AbstractLinkedListJava21.html#L607">607</a></td></tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2">
<pre>}

    /**
     * Inserts a new node into the list.
     *
     * @param nodeToInsert  new node to insert
     * @param insertBeforeNode  node to insert before
     * @throws NullPointerException if either node is null
     */
    protected void addNode(final Node&lt;E&gt; nodeToInsert, final Node&lt;E&gt; insertBeforeNode) {
        Objects.requireNonNull(nodeToInsert, "nodeToInsert");
        Objects.requireNonNull(insertBeforeNode, "insertBeforeNode");
        nodeToInsert.next = insertBeforeNode;
        nodeToInsert.previous = insertBeforeNode.previous;
        insertBeforeNode.previous.next = nodeToInsert;
        insertBeforeNode.previous = nodeToInsert;
        size++;
        modCount++;
    }

    /**
     * Creates a new node with the specified object as its
     * {@code value} and inserts it after {@code node}.
     * &lt;p&gt;
     * This implementation uses {@link #createNode(Object)} and
     * {@link #addNode(AbstractLinkedList.Node,AbstractLinkedList.Node)}.
     *
     * @param node  node to insert after
     * @param value  value of the newly added node
     * @throws NullPointerException if {@code node} is null
     */
    protected void addNodeAfter(final Node&lt;E&gt; node, final E value) {
        final Node&lt;E&gt; newNode = createNode(value);
        addNode(newNode, node.next);
    }

    /**
     * Creates a new node with the specified object as its
     * {@code value} and inserts it before {@code node}.
     * &lt;p&gt;
     * This implementation uses {@link #createNode(Object)} and
     * {@link #addNode(AbstractLinkedList.Node,AbstractLinkedList.Node)}.
     *
     * @param node  node to insert before
     * @param value  value of the newly added node
     * @throws NullPointerException if {@code node} is null
     */
    protected void addNodeBefore(final Node&lt;E&gt; node, final E value) {
        final Node&lt;E&gt; newNode = createNode(value);
        addNode(newNode, node);
    }

    @Override
    public void clear() {
        removeAllNodes();
    }

    @Override
    public boolean contains(final Object value) {
        return indexOf(value) != -1;
    }

    @Override
    public boolean containsAll(final Collection&lt;?&gt; coll) {
        for (final Object o : coll) {
            if (!contains(o)) {
                return false;
            }
        }
        return true;
    }

    /**
     * Creates a new node with previous, next and element all set to null.
     * This implementation creates a new empty Node.
     * Subclasses can override this to create a different class.
     *
     * @return  newly created node
     */
    protected Node&lt;E&gt; createHeaderNode() {
        return new Node&lt;&gt;();
    }

    /**
     * Creates a new node with the specified properties.
     * This implementation creates a new Node with data.
     * Subclasses can override this to create a different class.
     *
     * @param value  value of the new node
     * @return a new node containing the value
     */
    protected Node&lt;E&gt; createNode(final E value) {
        return new Node&lt;&gt;(value);
    }

    /**
     * Creates an iterator for the sublist.
     *
     * @param subList  the sublist to get an iterator for
     * @return a new iterator on the given sublist
     */
    protected Iterator&lt;E&gt; createSubListIterator(final LinkedSubList&lt;E&gt; subList) {
        return createSubListListIterator(subList, 0);
    }

    /**
     * Creates a list iterator for the sublist.
     *
     * @param subList  the sublist to get an iterator for
     * @param fromIndex  the index to start from, relative to the sublist
     * @return a new list iterator on the given sublist
     */
    protected ListIterator&lt;E&gt; createSubListListIterator(final LinkedSubList&lt;E&gt; subList, final int fromIndex) {
        return new LinkedSubListIterator&lt;&gt;(subList, fromIndex);
    }

    /**
     * Deserializes the data held in this object to the stream specified.
     * &lt;p&gt;
     * The first serializable subclass must call this method from
     * {@code readObject}.
     *
     * @param inputStream  the stream to read the object from
     * @throws IOException  if any error occurs while reading from the stream
     * @throws ClassNotFoundException  if a class read from the stream cannot be loaded
     */
    @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
    protected void doReadObject(final ObjectInputStream inputStream) throws IOException, ClassNotFoundException {
        init();
        final int size = inputStream.readInt();
        for (int i = 0; i &lt; size; i++) {
            add((E) inputStream.readObject());
        }
    }

    /**
     * Serializes the data held in this object to the stream specified.
     * &lt;p&gt;
     * The first serializable subclass must call this method from
     * {@code writeObject}.
     *
     * @param outputStream  the stream to write the object to
     * @throws IOException  if anything goes wrong
     */
    protected void doWriteObject(final ObjectOutputStream outputStream) throws IOException {
        // Write the size so we know how many nodes to read back
        outputStream.writeInt(size());
        for (final E e : this) {
            outputStream.writeObject(e);
        }
    }

    @Override
    public boolean equals(final Object obj) {
        if (obj == this) {
            return true;
        }
        if (!(obj instanceof List)) {
            return false;
        }
        final List&lt;?&gt; other = (List&lt;?&gt;) obj;
        if (other.size() != size()) {
            return false;
        }
        final ListIterator&lt;?&gt; it1 = listIterator();
        final ListIterator&lt;?&gt; it2 = other.listIterator();
        while (it1.hasNext() &amp;&amp; it2.hasNext()) {
            if (!Objects.equals(it1.next(), it2.next())) {
                return false;
            }
        }
        return !(it1.hasNext() || it2.hasNext());
    }

    @Override
    public E get(final int index) {
        final Node&lt;E&gt; node = getNode(index, false);
        return node.getValue();
    }

    /**
     * Gets the first element.
     *
     * @return the first element.
     */
    public E getFirst() {
        final Node&lt;E&gt; node = header.next;
        if (node == header) {
            throw new NoSuchElementException();
        }
        return node.getValue();
    }

    /**
     * Gets the last element.
     *
     * @return the last element.
     */
    public E getLast() {
        final Node&lt;E&gt; node = header.previous;
        if (node == header) {
            throw new NoSuchElementException();
        }
        return node.getValue();
    }

    /**
     * Gets the node at a particular index.
     *
     * @param index  the index, starting from 0
     * @param endMarkerAllowed  whether or not the end marker can be returned if
     * startIndex is set to the list's size
     * @return the node at the given index
     * @throws IndexOutOfBoundsException if the index is less than 0; equal to
     * the size of the list and endMakerAllowed is false; or greater than the
     * size of the list
     */
    protected Node&lt;E&gt; getNode(final int index, final boolean endMarkerAllowed) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException {
        // Check the index is within the bounds
        if (index &lt; 0) {
            throw new IndexOutOfBoundsException("Couldn't get the node: " +
                    "index (" + index + ") less than zero.");
        }
        if (!endMarkerAllowed &amp;&amp; index == size) {
            throw new IndexOutOfBoundsException("Couldn't get the node: " +
                    "index (" + index + ") is the size of the list.");
        }
        if (index &gt; size) {
            throw new IndexOutOfBoundsException("Couldn't get the node: " +
                    "index (" + index + ") greater than the size of the " +
                    "list (" + size + ").");
        }
        // Search the list and get the node
        Node&lt;E&gt; node;
        if (index &lt; size / 2) {
            // Search forwards
            node = header.next;
            for (int currentIndex = 0; currentIndex &lt; index; currentIndex++) {
                node = node.next;
            }
        } else {
            // Search backwards
            node = header;
            for (int currentIndex = size; currentIndex &gt; index; currentIndex--) {
                node = node.previous;
            }
        }
        return node;
    }

    @Override
    public int hashCode() {
        int hashCode = 1;
        for (final E e : this) {
            hashCode = 31 * hashCode + (e == null ? 0 : e.hashCode());
        }
        return hashCode;
    }

    @Override
    public int indexOf(final Object value) {
        int i = 0;
        for (Node&lt;E&gt; node = header.next; node != header; node = node.next) {
            if (isEqualValue(node.getValue(), value)) {
                return i;
            }
            i++;
        }
        return CollectionUtils.INDEX_NOT_FOUND;
    }

    /**
     * The equivalent of a default constructor, broken out so it can be called
     * by any constructor and by {@code readObject}.
     * Subclasses which override this method should make sure they call super,
     * so the list is initialized properly.
     */
    protected void init() {
        header = createHeaderNode();
    }

    @Override
    public boolean isEmpty() {
        return size() == 0;
    }

    /**
     * Compares two values for equals.
     * This implementation uses the equals method.
     * Subclasses can override this to match differently.
     *
     * @param value1  the first value to compare, may be null
     * @param value2  the second value to compare, may be null
     * @return true if equal
     */
    protected boolean isEqualValue(final Object value1, final Object value2) {
        return Objects.equals(value1, value2);
    }

    @Override
    public Iterator&lt;E&gt; iterator() {
        return listIterator();
    }

    @Override
    public int lastIndexOf(final Object value) {
        int i = size - 1;
        for (Node&lt;E&gt; node = header.previous; node != header; node = node.previous) {
            if (isEqualValue(node.getValue(), value)) {
                return i;
            }
            i--;
        }
        return CollectionUtils.INDEX_NOT_FOUND;
    }

    @Override
    public ListIterator&lt;E&gt; listIterator() {
        return new LinkedListIterator&lt;&gt;(this, 0);
    }

    @Override
    public ListIterator&lt;E&gt; listIterator(final int fromIndex) {
        return new LinkedListIterator&lt;&gt;(this, fromIndex);
    }

    @Override
    public E remove(final int index) {
        final Node&lt;E&gt; node = getNode(index, false);
        final E oldValue = node.getValue();
        removeNode(node);
        return oldValue;
    }

    @Override
    public boolean remove(final Object value) {
        for (Node&lt;E&gt; node = header.next; node != header; node = node.next) {
            if (isEqualValue(node.getValue(), value)) {
                removeNode(node);
                return true;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

    /**
     * {@inheritDoc}
     * &lt;p&gt;
     * This implementation iterates over the elements of this list, checking each element in
     * turn to see if it's contained in {@code coll}. If it's contained, it's removed
     * from this list. As a consequence, it is advised to use a collection type for
     * {@code coll} that provides a fast (for example O(1)) implementation of
     * {@link Collection#contains(Object)}.
     */
    @Override
    public boolean removeAll(final Collection&lt;?&gt; coll) {
        boolean modified = false;
        final Iterator&lt;E&gt; it = iterator();
        while (it.hasNext()) {
            if (coll.contains(it.next())) {
                it.remove();
                modified = true;
            }
        }
        return modified;
    }

    /**
     * Removes all nodes by resetting the circular list marker.
     */
    protected void removeAllNodes() {
        header.next = header;
        header.previous = header;
        size = 0;
        modCount++;
    }

    /**
     * Removes the first element.
     *
     * @return The value removed.
     */
    public E removeFirst() {
        final Node&lt;E&gt; node = header.next;
        if (node == header) {
            throw new NoSuchElementException();
        }
        final E oldValue = node.getValue();
        removeNode(node);
        return oldValue;
    }

    /**
     * Removes the last element.
     *
     * @return The value removed.
     */
    public E removeLast() {
        final Node&lt;E&gt; node = header.previous;
        if (node == header) {
            throw new NoSuchElementException();
        }
        final E oldValue = node.getValue();
        removeNode(node);
        return oldValue;
    }

    /**
     * Removes the specified node from the list.
     *
     * @param node  the node to remove
     * @throws NullPointerException if {@code node} is null
     */
    protected void removeNode(final Node&lt;E&gt; node) {
        Objects.requireNonNull(node, "node");
        node.previous.next = node.next;
        node.next.previous = node.previous;
        size--;
        modCount++;
    }

    /**
     * {@inheritDoc}
     * &lt;p&gt;
     * This implementation iterates over the elements of this list, checking each element in
     * turn to see if it's contained in {@code coll}. If it's not contained, it's removed
     * from this list. As a consequence, it is advised to use a collection type for
     * {@code coll} that provides a fast (for example O(1)) implementation of
     * {@link Collection#contains(Object)}.
     */
    @Override
    public boolean retainAll(final Collection&lt;?&gt; coll) {
        boolean modified = false;
        final Iterator&lt;E&gt; it = iterator();
        while (it.hasNext()) {
            if (!coll.contains(it.next())) {
                it.remove();
                modified = true;
            }
        }
        return modified;
    }

    @Override
    public E set(final int index, final E value) {
        final Node&lt;E&gt; node = getNode(index, false);
        final E oldValue = node.getValue();
        updateNode(node, value);
        return oldValue;
    }

    @Override
    public int size() {
        return size;
    }

    /**
     * Gets a sublist of the main list.
     *
     * @param fromIndexInclusive  the index to start from
     * @param toIndexExclusive  the index to end at
     * @return the new sublist
     */
    @Override
    public List&lt;E&gt; subList(final int fromIndexInclusive, final int toIndexExclusive) {
        return new LinkedSubList&lt;&gt;(this, fromIndexInclusive, toIndexExclusive);
    }

    @Override
    public Object[] toArray() {
        return toArray(new Object[size]);
    }

    @Override
    @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
    public &lt;T&gt; T[] toArray(T[] array) {
        // Extend the array if needed
        if (array.length &lt; size) {
            final Class&lt;?&gt; componentType = array.getClass().getComponentType();
            array = (T[]) Array.newInstance(componentType, size);
        }
        // Copy the values into the array
        int i = 0;
        for (Node&lt;E&gt; node = header.next; node != header; node = node.next, i++) {
            array[i] = (T) node.getValue();
        }
        // Set the value after the last value to null
        if (array.length &gt; size) {
            array[size] = null;
        }
        return array;
    }

    @Override
    public String toString() {
        if (isEmpty()) {
            return "[]";
        }
        final StringBuilder buf = new StringBuilder(16 * size());
        buf.append(CollectionUtils.DEFAULT_TOSTRING_PREFIX);

        final Iterator&lt;E&gt; it = iterator();
        boolean hasNext = it.hasNext();
        while (hasNext) {
            final Object value = it.next();
            buf.append(value == this ? "(this Collection)" : value);
            hasNext = it.hasNext();
            if (hasNext) {
                buf.append(", ");
            }
        }
        buf.append(CollectionUtils.DEFAULT_TOSTRING_SUFFIX);
        return buf.toString();
    }

    /**
     * Updates the node with a new value.
     * This implementation sets the value on the node.
     * Subclasses can override this to record the change.
     *
     * @param node  node to update
     * @param value  new value of the node
     */
    protected void updateNode(final Node&lt;E&gt; node, final E value) {
        node.setValue(value);
    }
